entities.name: FINANCIAL PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
entities.description: Financial tools and services whose demand and user experiences are being studied among smallholder farmers and enterprises

entities.name: CLIMATE SMART VILLAGES
entities.description: Localized areas where climate smart agricultural technologies and practices are implemented and studied

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: Small-scale agricultural producers who use financial services and climate smart technologies

entities.name: CLIMATE SMART AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGIES AND PRACTICES
entities.description: Technical methods and agricultural practices designed to increase productivity and resilience to climate change

entities.name: CLIMATE SMART AGRICULTURE
entities.description: An integrated approach to managing landscapes to address food security and climate change, often abbreviated as CSA

entities.name: CCAFS
entities.description: The CGIAR Research Program on Climate Change, Agriculture and Food Security, which supports climate smart projects

entities.name: SURVEY
entities.description: A research method used to identify farmers and enterprises and explore their demand for financial services

entities.name: SMALL TO MEDIUM ENTERPRISES
entities.description: Businesses within the agricultural value chain surveyed for their experiences with financial products

entities.name: LATIN AMERICA
entities.description: A geographic region where the survey was conducted

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A geographic region where the survey was conducted

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country in South Asia where the role of female farmers in irrigated agriculture was noted as limited

entities.name: IRRIGATED AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A form of farming that uses controlled application of water, mentioned in the context of female farmer roles in India

entities.name: ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of implementing climate smart agriculture, though the extent of this process was not defined in the study
